services.name: User Keybag Services via Mailbox
services.description: Step 1. The module receives User credential and the reference to the class C or A key from the User keybag Step 2. Obfuscation operation is performed on the User provided credential resulting into a value called REK. Step 3. REK is used as a key for the AES KW operation to unwrap the referenced class A or C keys in the user keybag stored in the module. Step 4. Status of unwrapping operation of class keys is returned via mailbox interface and the REK is zeroised
services.cspsAccessed: User credential, REK, User keybag (Class A key, Class AK key, Class AKU key, Class AKPU key, Class C key, Class CK key, Class CKU key)

services.name: General Authentication service
services.description: The module invokes the User keybag Services via Mailbox (i.e. #1 above)
services.cspsAccessed: User credential, REK, User keybag (Class A key, Class AK key, Class AKU key, Class AKPU key, Class C key, Class CK key, Class CKU key)

services.name: Generation of Data Encryption Key (DEK)
services.description: Step 1: The module receives the reference to the class C or A key from the user keybag Step 2: The module generates a new DEK using the DRBG Step 3: Referenced class C or A key is used to wrap the DEK using AES-KW Step 4: Wrapped DEK is sent out of the module
services.cspsAccessed: Entropy input string, DRBG internal state
services.approvedFunctions: Symmetric Key Generation (CKG using method in Section 4 example 1 [SP 800- 133Rev2], AES- ECB, AES-CBC) Key Wrapping: AES-KW

services.name: Wrapped DEK
Keychain DEK service using AK/ AKU/ AKPU/ CK/ CKU class key
services.description: Step 1. The module receives wrapped DEK (that was sent as part of service 3 above) and the pointer to class key AK/ AKU/AKPU/CK/ CKU from the user keybag. Step 2. Using the referenced class key, the module unwraps the DEK using AES-KW. If the class key is not available, an error is returned. Step 3. plaintext DEK is sent out to the User.

Backup keybag service
services.description: Step 1. The module receives wrapped DEK and the class key reference for C and A from the user keybag. Step 2. Using the referenced class key, the module unwraps the DEK using AES-KW. If the class key is not available, an error is returned. Step 3. The module generates a set of backup key bag using DRBG Step 4. Unwrapped DEK is re-wrapped with

services.approvedFunctions: Key Wrapping and Unwrapping: AES- KW Symmetric Key Generation (CKG using method in Section 4 example 1 [SP 800- 133Rev2], AES- ECB, AES-CBC)

services.description: backup key bag key using AES-KW Step 5. Wrapped DEK is sent out.

services.name: Escrow keybag (Class A key, Class AK key, Class AKU key, Class AKPU key, Class C key)
Export Keybag
services.description: Step 1. The module receives reference to a keybag. Step 2: A HMAC key is taken as input based on the hardware specific data for the SKS Step 3: HMAC value is calculated on the entire referenced keybag that includes encrypted4 keys. Step 4: HMAC is appended at the end of the keybag Step 5: Keybag with the appended HMAC is output to the User

services.description: Wrapping of provided plaintext DEK or unwrapping of provided wrapped DEK using class D key from Backup keybag or secure storage in SEP

services.description: Encryption of provided plaintext or decryption of provided ciphertext using class D key from Device or iCloud Keybag

services.description: Wrapping of provided plaintext keychain or unwrapping of provided wrapped keychain using class DK/DKU key from Backup keybag or User keybag

services.description: Encryption of provided plaintext or decryption of provided ciphertext using DK/DKU key from Device or iCloud keybag

services.description: Key Generation

services.name: Sign and verify using Ref-key
services.description: Signature Generation and Verification

services.name: Encryption and decryption using Ref-key
services.description: shared secret is generated using user provided key and existing ref key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used to encrypt the provided plaintext or decrypt the provided ciphertext

services.name: Generate Shared Secret using Ref-key
services.description: Shared secret generation

services.name: Device keybag service for data encrypt or decrypt
services.description: Encryption of provided plaintext or decryption of provided ciphertext using any key from Device keybag

services.name: iCloud keybag service for data encrypt or decrypt
services.description: Encryption of provided plaintext or decryption of provided ciphertext using any key from iCloud keybag

services.name: Escrow keybag service for key wrapping and unwrapping
services.description: Wrapping of provided plaintext key or unwrapping of provided wrapped key using any key from Escrow keybag

services.name: Encrypt or Decrypt service using Class B Curve 22519 key from any key bag
services.description: shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used for data encryption or decryption. During encryption operations, the wrapped key and the ephemeral public key are sent to the user

services.name: Wrap or unwrap service for DEK or keychain using any Curve 22519 key from asymmetric key bag
services.description: shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used to wrap and unwrap DEK or keychain. During wrapping operation, the wrapped key and the ephemeral public key are sent to the user

services.name: Asymmetric (Ed25519) backup keybag wrap and unwrap
services.description: shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used to wrap and unwrap. The wrapped key and the ephemeral public key are sent to the user

services.name: Wrap or unwrap service for keychain using DK/DKU/CK/ CKU/AK/AKU/AKPU Ed25519 key from asymmetric key bag (Non-approved)
services.description: Pointer to DK/DKU/CK/CKU/AK/AKU/AKPU key from asymmetric keybag, plaintext keychain during wrapping operation or wrapped keychain during unwrapping operation

services.description: wrapping DEK using NVM storage controller key

services.name: PBKDF Key Derivation
services.description: Hash-based Key Derivation

services.name: File system DEK service
services.description: Unwrap the DEK using referenced class key and re-wrap using NVM storage controller key

Details
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.keySize: Minimum number of bits of entropy

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: The entropy source consists of twenty-four Free Ring Oscillator (FROs). The entropy source has been shown to provide full 256-bits of entropy at the output of the vetted conditioning function, SHA2-256 (#C1223).
HMAC-SHA256
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: CAST (KAT) performed prior to module’s firmware integrity test at power-up

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: AES Implementations AES-KW, AES-CBC, AES-ECB using 128-bit key
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Separate encryption / decryption CAST (KAT) performed for each mode

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: CTR_DRBG with 256-bit key
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: CAST (KAT) performed CAST: Health test per SP800-90ARev1 section 11.3

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: Physical entropy source
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: SP800-90B health test (APT and RCT) classified as CAST: - at start-up: performed on 1,024 consecutive samples. - during runtime.

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: Failed Pre-operational Software Integrity Test
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Error message “FAILED: fipspost_post_integrity” sent to caller

approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.name: Failed CAST
approvedAlgorithmsDetailed.useFunction: Error message “FAILED:<event>” sent to caller (<event> refers to any of the cryptographic functions listed in Table 15)

Obfuscation operation is performed on the User provided credential resulting into a value called REK. Step 3. REK is used as a key for the AES KW operation to unwrap the referenced class A or C keys in the user keybag stored in the module. Step 4. Status of unwrapping operation of class keys is returned via mailbox interface and the REK is zeroised

Step 1. The module receives reference to a keybag. Step 2: A HMAC key is taken as input based on the hardware specific data for the SKS Step 3: HMAC value is calculated on the entire referenced keybag that includes encrypted4 keys. Step 4: HMAC is appended at the end of the keybag Step 5: Keybag with the appended HMAC is output to the User

Encrypt or Decrypt service using Class B Curve 22519 key from any key bag

shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used for data encryption or decryption. During encryption operations, the wrapped key and the ephemeral public key are sent to the user
Wrap or unwrap service for DEK or keychain using any Curve 22519 key from asymmetric key bag

shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used to wrap and unwrap DEK or keychain. During wrapping operation, the wrapped key and the ephemeral public key are sent to the user
Asymmetric (Ed25519) backup keybag wrap and unwrap

shared secret is computed by generating new ephemeral keypair and existing Curve25519 key followed by HKDF is applied to derive a key which is used to wrap and unwrap. The wrapped key and the ephemeral public key are sent to the user

Minimum number of bits of entropy

The entropy source consists of twenty-four Free Ring Oscillator (FROs). The entropy source has been shown to provide full 256-bits of entropy at the output of the vetted conditioning function, SHA2-256 (#C1223).
